questions.question: Which two permissions must a Pages-deploying workflow grant in addition to the default `contents: read`?
questions.explanation: `pages: write` is required for `deploy-pages` to publish; `id-token: write` is required because the deploy action exchanges an OIDC token for short-lived credentials. Without either, the deploy step fails with 'Resource not accessible by integration.'

questions.question: You push three commits within a minute. Your workflow has no `concurrency` block. What happens?
choices: Only the first commit deploys; the others queue and never run
All three runs execute in parallel and the slower one may overwrite the latest
GitHub aborts the workflow with an error about overlapping runs
Actions auto-collapses them into a single combined run
questions.explanation: Without a `concurrency` group, multiple runs proceed in parallel. Whichever finishes last wins the deploy slot, which can mean an older commit ends up live. A `concurrency: { group: pages, cancel-in-progress: true }` block prevents that.

questions.question: You set deploy source to 'GitHub Actions' and your workflow's `build` step does not set `JEKYLL_ENV`. Module 4 wrapped the analytics snippet in `{% if jekyll.environment == "production" %}`. What is the trade-off you've created?
choices: Analytics will fire twice in production
The build will fail because `jekyll.environment` is undefined
Analytics will not fire because `jekyll.environment` defaults to `"development"`, so the production-only include is skipped
Pages will refuse the artifact because production builds are required
questions.explanation: Without an explicit `JEKYLL_ENV`, Jekyll defaults to `development`. Any template guarded on `jekyll.environment == "production"` is skipped, so analytics, minification, and similar prod-only includes never run. Set `env: JEKYLL_ENV: production` on the build step.
